title: Saarai (Chagrian)

{{Quote|My line continues with a daughter in training on Korriban. She will be a fine Wyyrlok in her time.|Darth Wyyrlok the Third, referring to Saarai|<ref name="Legacy 14" />}}
'''Saarai''', a [[Chagrian/Legends|Chagrian]] [[Gender/Legends|female]], was a [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]] and the daughter of the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Darth Wyyrlok (III)|Darth Wyyrlok]], who was the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]] [[Darth Krayt]]'s [[Voice]] and [[Regent/Legends|second-in-command]] of the [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|Empire]] and [[One Sith]]. She was also expected to become the fourth Chagrian to have the name Wyyrlok bestowed upon her. In [[137 ABY]], after Darth Krayt's [[Battle on Had Abbadon|apparent death]] on [[Had Abbadon]]—Saarai's [[Parent/Legends|father]] actually murdered Krayt after the latter sustained severe injuries from a battle with a group led by former [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Cade Skywalker]]—Saarai guarded Krayt's [[Stasis Room|stasis chamber]] in the ancient Sith Lord [[XoXaan]]'s [[XoXaan's Temple|temple]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]], per her father's instructions to not allow anyone entrance. When Saarai was confronted by [[Darth Stryfe]], one of Krayt's [[Hand (One Sith)|Hands]] who participated in the battle in which Krayt was seemingly killed, she managed to successfully keep Stryfe from entering the chamber. Saarai's father intervened before she could [[Death/Legends|kill]] Stryfe.

A few [[Standard month/Legends|months]] later, in [[138 ABY]], Saarai and the One Sith felt the call of Darth Krayt through the Force, revealing that he was alive and not in stasis despite Wyyrlok's claims. After her father openly admitted to usurping Krayt, Saarai left the galactic capital [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] per his instructions, because her power had not yet been fully developed. Although her father was slain by Krayt in their [[Assault on the Temple of the Sith|confrontation]], Saarai continued to serve the Sith Order, even after Krayt himself was [[Battle of Coruscant (Second Imperial Civil War)|killed]] by Cade Skywalker and the Sith were defeated by the combined efforts of the forces of deposed [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Roan Fel]], [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gar Stazi]], and the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]]. After the establishment of the [[Galactic Federation Triumvirate]], Saarai operated as a Sith [[Agent/Legends|agent]] who, under the leadership of [[Darth Nihl]], would infiltrate the galactic governments in an effort to destroy the One Sith Order's enemies from within.

==Biography==
{{Quote|Saarai is my daughter and will, some day, be the next Darth Wyyrlok.|Darth Wyyrlok III|<ref name="Legacy 34" />}}
The [[Chagrian/Legends|Chagrian]] [[Gender/Legends|female]]<ref name="Legacy 14" /> [[Saarai]]<ref name="Legacy 34" /> was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] to the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Darth Wyyrlok (III)|Darth Wyyrlok]], the third in a line of Chagrian [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Lord/Legends|Lords]] named Wyyrlok, in the [[Time/Legends|time]] period of the [[Sith–Imperial War]]. From her [[Youngling/Legends|youth]], Saarai was [[Sith training|instructed]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]] in the ways of the [[One Sith]] Order, whose leader was the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Darth Krayt]], also the [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] of the [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="Legacy 14" /> After Wyyrlok secretly [[Battle on Had Abbadon|rendered]] Darth Krayt inactive, Saarai was placed in charge of guarding [[Stasis Room|Krayt's tomb]] within [[XoXaan's Temple]] while her father prepared it.

[[File:WyyrlokIV_Stryfe.jpg|thumb|left|175px|Saarai fighting Darth Stryfe with telekinesis.]]
When a recently revived [[Darth Stryfe]] demanded entrance to the tomb, she refused to allow him to enter. Stryfe attacked her, and after a protracted unarmed fight, Saarai overpowered the experienced Sith Lord. Using her considerable Force talents, she literally had his body bending to her will. However, her father came out and ordered her to cease before she could kill Stryfe. Then, he had Saarai return to her duty and let Stryfe enter the Stasis Room so they could discuss Krayt's "serious condition."

Some time later, Saarai transferred to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. She was present at her father's side when the resurrected Darth Krayt made his presence and will felt by all Sith through the dark side of the Force. However, once word was received that Darth Krayt was on his way to Coruscant, Darth Wyyrlok convinced his hesitant daughter to flee the planet since her powers were not fully realized. Saarai was not present when Lord Krayt [[Assault on the Temple of the Sith|marched]] into the [[Temple of the Sith]] and killed her father in a duel. Months later, after the [[Battle of Coruscant (Second Imperial Civil War)|death]] of Darth Krayt and the collapse of the [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire|Sith Empire]], Saarai went into hiding among the galactic populace. She was following the orders of Darth Nihl, successor of Lord Krayt, who ordered the Sith to disperse and infiltrate the various governments throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] and await the time for their revenge.

==Personality and traits==
[[File:Wyyrlok_dynasty.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Saarai with her father, Darth Wyyrlok III.]]
Saarai appeared to have a close bond with her father and was reluctant to leave him when the resurrected Darth Krayt attacked the Temple of the Sith. She also refused to allow Darth Stryfe to enter Darth Krayt's tomb and despite being a Sith apprentice she was willing to fight him to stop him from entering. It was only after Darth Wyyrlok commanded her to stop using her telekinetic powers that she allowed Stryfe to enter. She remained loyal to the One Sith and, after Krayt's death, continued to serve the new order under [[Darth Nihl]]. Her given name in the Sith language meant "truth."<ref name="Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/88/indexp2.html|text=''Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties''|int=Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties}}</ref>

==Powers and abilities==
{{Quote|I have your heart in my hand.|Saarai to Darth Stryfe|<ref name="Legacy 34" />}}
Even though she was merely an "unmarked" Sith apprentice, Saarai was highly proficient in the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]]. She was highly skilled with [[Telekinesis/Legends|telekinesis]], being capable of overpowering a Sith Lord as powerful as Darth Stryfe, who had served as both the [[Fist]] and a [[Hand (One Sith)|Hand]], able to employ the Force to exert pressure on Lord Stryfe's heart, and then use the Force to twist his muscular body.

==Behind the scenes==
Saarai was created by [[John Ostrander]] for the ''[[Star Wars: Legacy]]'' [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] series, and was first mentioned by her father, the third Darth Wyyrlok, in the [[Legacy (2006) 14|fourteenth issue]]. She made her first appearance and was first identified in the ''Legacy'' in the [[Legacy (2006) 34|thirty-fourth issue]], which was the first issue of the [[Star Wars: Legacy: Storms|''Storms'']] story arc. She later was featured in ''Legacy''{{'s}} [[Legacy (2006) 50|fiftieth and final issue]], and also appeared in the [[Legacy — War 1|first]] and [[Legacy — War 6|sixth]] issues of the follow-up miniseries [[Star Wars: Legacy — War|''Star Wars: Legacy—War'']]. Saarai was drawn by [[Jan Duursema]] for all of her appearances in ''Legacy'' and ''Legacy—War''. She was mentioned in her father's entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', which was released in [[2008]].
